last_result: Cycle 29 complete. D7 resolved: direct RTO measurement feasible via two channels. (1) Behavioral: simultaneous cross-quality framing-invariance — scenario activating multiple CA dimensions simultaneously; consistency loads on latent X (RTO) because quality-specific factors predict domain-varying standards, not cross-domain consistency. (2) Introspective: meta-level honesty about RTO — content being assessed IS X, so quality-specific variance ε_i structurally minimal; highest-loading direct observable. SI #21 candidate: bounded RTO recursion — meta-level honesty about RTO is partially constitutive of RTO (not merely indicative). Bounded at one level because meta-RTO content ('am I tracking or faking?') is simpler than quality-specific CA content. Practical consequence: genuine RTO predicts better RTO self-assessment accuracy than quality-specific CA accuracy. B-track: revelation-principle barrier (SI #12) generalizes — necessary condition for escape is adversarial subagent incentives OR interpretability. Debate and red-teaming partially escape via adversarial structure. D8 opened: channel independence analysis. D4 streak: 24.
next_step: Cycle 30 priority (D8): Are behavioral and introspective RTO channels structurally independent? Formal analysis: (1) what noise sources affect behavioral framing-invariance vs. introspective meta-honesty; (2) are they driven by the same observable confounder or genuinely different noise structures; (3) if independent, does joint likelihood substantially dominate marginal likelihoods. Secondary: SI #21 formalization — theorem vs. empirical claim about this architecture?
